records.specific_details: Annual National Household Travel Survey
records.intended_results: To reconsider proceeding with this outdated method, https://www.etenders.gov.ie/epps/cft/prepareViewCfTWS.do?resourceId=5530425 I propose leveraging anonymized mobile phone GPS tracking data combined with AI-driven analysis to create a more robust, scalable, and real-time solution for transport planning and management. ::Educate TDs on the near-future possibilities with AI traffic management, as mentioned during the media debate about Metrolink, highlighted by Dermot Desmond at an AI conference in Belfast https://www.irishtimes.com/ireland/dublin/2025/08/05/dublins-planned-metrolink-will-be-obsolete-because-of-artificial-intelligence-says-dermot-desmond/

To appear before the new AI, Transport and Infrastructure committees. - Phone call

records.lobbyist_name: An Taisce The National Trust for Ireland
records.specific_details: New EU Multi-annual financial framework, MFF for EU 27 Heads of State EU Council Meeting 26-27 June 2025
records.intended_results: To highlight concerns of the EEB on proposals for the MFF, and lack of ringfencing of funding for nature, and on the negative impacts of the proposals to Life+ funding and consequential impacts for nature and civil society organisations capacity, and to seek awareness and pushback.::To highlight support for the concerns and recommendations  in the EEB letter to HoS for the EU Council meeting on 26-27 June 2025, and to further emphasise: the implications for Ireland's upcomming Presidency of the EU; the security risks inherent in a failure to address the triple planetary crisis, risks associated with destabilisation of our democracies and further alienation of society & undermining of the rule of law and public accountability::the need to address misinformation and to provide high quality information; grave concern on the failure to uphold international law, and to address our obligations as third States in the face of serious breaches of international law; call for pressure to invoke A
lobbying_activities: Email
Informal communication

lobbying_activities: Emailed Housing Minister James Browne's office on [6 May] to follow up on request sent (25 April) inviting him to address the launch of the Home User Guide at the Healthy Homes Ireland Forum on Tuesday 27 May at Buswells Hotel. Requested that the invite be forwarded on to another Junior Minister in DHLGH if Minister Browne was unable to attend. - Email

Emailed Minister of State Kieran O'Donnell [13 May,19 May, 23 May] to follow up on request for him to address the launch of the Home User Guide at the Healthy Homes Ireland Forum on Tuesday 27 May. - Email

Emailed several DPOs on [4 June] who had been invited to, but were unable to attend the Healthy Homes Ireland Forum breakfast event to provide them with a brief recap of the event and attach some photos. - Email

Emailed the office of Minister of State John Cummins on the [7 May] to thank them for considering our request for Minister Cummins to address the launch of the Home User Guide at the Healthy Homes Ireland Forum on Tuesday 27 May. Requested that the Minister of State for Older People and Housing Kieran O'Donnell. - Email

Held an event in Buswells Hotel on [May 27] with over 40 attendees. The Healthy Homes Ireland Forum breakfast event brought together key stakeholders to consider how occupants can be empowered to operate healthy and energy efficient homes. The Irish Green Building Council's Home User Guide was launched at the event and there followed a panel discussion with experts on the importance of a healthy indoor environment and how householders can be empowered to run a healthy home. - Event / Reception
On [10 June, 9 July] emailed Colm Burke TD to request a meeting with him to discuss the recommendations Healthy Homes Ireland's 2023 report; 'Our place: Towards Healthier Greener Homes’. - Email

On [12 June] emailed Micheal Carrigy TD, Chair of the  Joint Oireachtas Committee on Housing, Local Government and Heritage to request a meeting with him to discuss the recommendations Healthy Homes Ireland's 2023 report; 'Our place: Towards Healthier Greener Homes’. - Email

On [12 June] emailed Naoise O' Muirí TD, Chair of the Joint Oireachtas Committee on Climate, Environment and Energy to request a meeting with him to discuss the recommendations Healthy Homes Ireland's 2023 report; 'Our place: Towards Healthier Greener Homes’. - Email
On [17 June] emailed Rory Hearne TD, to request a meeting with him to discuss the recommendations Healthy Homes Ireland's 2023 report; 'Our place: Towards Healthier Greener Homes’. - Email

On [18 June, 30 July, 4 August, 6 August] emailed Pa Daly TD to request a meeting with her to discuss the recommendations Healthy Homes Ireland's 2023 report; 'Our place: Towards Healthier Greener Homes’. - Email
On [18 June] emailed Conor Sheehan TD to request a meeting with him to discuss the recommendations Healthy Homes Ireland's 2023 report; 'Our place: Towards Healthier Greener Homes’. - Email
On [18 June] emailed Jen Cummins TD to request a meeting with her to discuss the recommendations Healthy Homes Ireland's 2023 report; 'Our place: Towards Healthier Greener Homes’. - Email

On [18 June] emailed Minister Jennifer Murnane O'Connor to request a meeting with her to discuss the recommendations in the Healthy Homes Ireland's 2023 report; 'Our place: Towards Healthier Greener Homes’. - Email

On [18 June] emailed Minister of State Kieran O'Donnell's office to request a meeting with him to discuss the recommendations Healthy Homes Ireland's 2023 report; 'Our place: Towards Healthier Greener Homes’. - Email

On [19 June] rang Jen Cummins TD's office and left a voicemail, requesting a meeting with her to discuss the recommendations Healthy Homes Ireland's 2023 report; 'Our place: Towards Healthier Greener Homes’. - Phone call

On [27 May] emailed Padraig Rice TD to request a meeting with him to discuss the recommendations Healthy Homes Ireland's 2023 report; 'Our place: Towards Healthier Greener Homes’. - Email

Rang the office of the Minister of State Kieran O'Donnell to follow up on request for him to address the launch of the Home User Guide at the Healthy Homes Ireland Forum on Tuesday 27 May. - Phone call

Reminders sent on [26 May] to several DPOs including TDs, Senators, civil and public servants from relevant government departments and agencies reminding them that they have been invited to attend the Healthy Homes Ireland Forum breakfast event in Buswells Hotel [27 May 2025]. The event marked the launch of the Home User Guide. - Email
